summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn continued the 'Do Something Radical' series with a Mother's Day message on radical sacrifice. He introduced the theme by sharing the church's vision to launch a second location through a $6 million cash project, calling the congregation to radical faith. The heart of the sermon was a live interview with his mother, Tony, whose life story illustrated profound sacrifice and God's redemptive power. She shared a childhood marked by fatherlessness, her mother's addiction and murder, and years of sexual abuse. As a teenage mother herself, she faced divorce, infidelity, and financial hardship, yet consistently chose forgiveness, hard work, and fierce dedication to her children. Pastor Travis highlighted how her radical, Christ-centered choices—like forgiving the woman her husband cheated with and helping raise their child—demonstrated the biblical call to lay down one's life. The message emphasized that pain, when yielded to God, produces spiritual depth and stability. He closed by reading from his mother's well-worn Bible, focusing on Isaiah 59:21 as a promise of generational blessing for those who build their lives on God's word. The sermon was a powerful call to sacrificial love, forgiveness, and building a legacy of faith, especially for mothers and families.
mainPoints: Radical sacrifice, modeled by Christ and faithful mothers, involves laying down one's life through forgiveness, perseverance, and selfless love.
Pain and trauma, when surrendered to God, can become catalysts for deep spiritual growth, stability, and redemptive testimony.
Building a life on the rock of Jesus Christ secures a legacy of generational blessing, breaking curses and establishing a faithful heritage.

versesMentioned.text: Remember this: Whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows generously will also reap generously.
Greater love has no one than this: to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.
Blows and wounds scrub away evil, and beatings purge the inmost being.

"As for me, this is my covenant with them," says the LORD. "My Spirit, who is on you, and my words that I have put in your mouth will not depart from your mouth, or from the mouths of your children, or from the mouths of their descendants from this time on and forever," says the LORD.
For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.
If you declare with your mouth, "Jesus is Lord," and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
Therefore, I urge you, brothers and sisters, in view of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God—this is your true and proper worship.

Monday.scripture: John 15:13 - "Greater love has no one than this: to lay down one’s life for one’s friends."
Monday.action: Identify one practical way you can put someone else's need before your own convenience today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to see opportunities to love others sacrificially, just as Jesus loved me.

Tuesday.scripture: Proverbs 20:30 - "Blows and wounds scrub away evil, and beatings purge the inmost being."
Tuesday.action: Reflect on a past hardship. Write down one way God used it to strengthen your character or faith.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, thank you that you redeem our pain. Help me to trust your refining work in my life.

Wednesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 9:6 - "Whoever sows generously will also reap generously."
Wednesday.focus: The principle of sowing and reaping.
Wednesday.action: Perform an act of generosity today—it could be with your time, resources, or encouragement—without expecting anything in return.
Wednesday.prayer: God, give me a generous heart. Let me sow seeds of kindness and faith into the lives around me.

Thursday.scripture: Romans 12:1 - "Offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God."
Thursday.action: Begin your day with a prayer of surrender, offering your plans, work, and relationships to God as an act of worship.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, I offer myself to you today as a living sacrifice. Use me for your purposes and glory.

Friday.scripture: Isaiah 59:21 - "My words... will not depart from your mouth, or from the mouths of your children..."
Friday.action: Pray specifically for the spiritual future of your children, grandchildren, or spiritual descendants. Speak a blessing over them.
Friday.prayer: Lord, establish your word in my mouth and in the mouths of my family for generations to come. Build a legacy of faith.

Saturday.scripture: Romans 10:9 - "If you declare... 'Jesus is Lord,' and believe... you will be saved."
Saturday.action: If you have never done so, confess Jesus as Lord. If you have, share this simple truth with someone in your life this weekend.
Saturday.prayer: God, thank you for the gift of salvation through Jesus. Give me boldness to confess Him and share this hope with others.
